fields.summary: Create an agent-less docker-less provider in the native services framework
[Umbrella] Native YARN framework layer for services and beyond

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: The goal of the agent-less docker-less provider is to be able to use the YARN native services framework when Docker is not installed or other methods of app resource installation are preferable.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Create an agent-less docker provider in the native-services framework

This message was automatically generated.
[~billie.rinaldi] the patch does not apply on latest yarn-native-services branch. It conflicts on DockerProviderService.java. Can you please refresh to latest in the branch?

Here is an updated patch that also introduces a simple default provider in addition to the new tarball provider. I am planning a follow-on patch that will change ServiceApiUtil to use the new client provider validation methods and to handle the APPLICATION artifact type. I'll add some tests in the follow-on patch as well. I could do this all in the same patch, but thought it might be better to break it up for easier reviewing.

looks good over all, minor comments:
- remove unused variable SliderAppMaster#providers ?
- the slider.xml as discussed offline may be removed. we'll add a new xml file for how to config native service framework when required.

Thanks for the review, [~jianhe]! Here is a new patch that removes the slider.xml config file that is no longer used and properly initializes the providers list in SliderAppMaster. I opened YARN-6613 for the follow-on validation work.

This message was automatically generated.
lgtm, for the findbugs warnings, what is the doClientInstall supposed to do ? If that's not required any more, may be we can just remove these code.

I think we will need to fix the client install. For a tarball, client install would download and untar the tarball, then create config files needed for a client installation of the app. For a docker app, maybe client install could create the client config files, then print out a sample docker run command that uses the config files to run an app client.
sounds good, I'll commit the current patch
committed to yarn-native-services, thanks Billie !

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #13193 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/13193/])

This message was automatically generated.
[~billie.rinaldi] the patch does not apply on latest yarn-native-services branch. It conflicts on DockerProviderService.java. Can you please refresh to latest in the branch?

Here is an updated patch that also introduces a simple default provider in addition to the new tarball provider. I am planning a follow-on patch that will change ServiceApiUtil to use the new client provider validation methods and to handle the APPLICATION artifact type. I'll add some tests in the follow-on patch as well. I could do this all in the same patch, but thought it might be better to break it up for easier reviewing.

This message was automatically generated.
lgtm, for the findbugs warnings, what is the doClientInstall supposed to do ? If that's not required any more, may be we can just remove these code.

I think we will need to fix the client install. For a tarball, client install would download and untar the tarball, then create config files needed for a client installation of the app. For a docker app, maybe client install could create the client config files, then print out a sample docker run command that uses the config files to run an app client.
sounds good, I'll commit the current patch
committed to yarn-native-services, thanks Billie !
